ObtainJSONWebTokenInput.description:
The input type for obtaining a Kraken Token (JWT).

The currently supported inputs are:
- account user email/password combination
- account user API key
- organization live secret key
- pre-signed key
- refresh token

AccountUser.description: User objects are the core of the authentication system. They typically represent a customer who manages a portfolio of one or more accounts.

fields.name: number
fields.description: A code that uniquely identifies the account user.

fields.name: accounts
fields.description: List of accounts that the user is linked to either via portfolio role or account role.

args.name: allowedBrandCodes
args.description: Optionally filter the user's accounts to only return those linked to portfolios on the specified brands.

args.name: restrictToPublicFacingBrands
args.description: Optionally restrict user accounts to only return those linked to portfolios on public facing brands.

args.name: restrictToAccountNumbers
args.description: Optionally restrict user accounts to only return those with the specified account numbers.

args.name: excludeAccountTypes
args.description: Optionally exclude accounts with any of the given account types.

args.name: excludeAccountsWithoutAgreements
args.description: Optionally exclude accounts that have never had an agreement.

fields.name: pronouns
fields.description: The user's pronouns e.g. 'she/her', 'he/him', 'they/them'.

fields.name: isDeceased
fields.description: Designates whether this user is deceased.

fields.name: liveSecretKey
fields.description: The user's secret key to access the Developer API.

fields.name: portfolios
fields.description: List of portfolios that the user is linked to via their portfolio roles.

args.name: allowedBrandCodes
args.description: Optionally filter the user's portfolios to only return those linked to specified brands.

args.name: restrictToPublicFacingBrands
args.description: Optionally restrict the user portfolios to only return those linked to public facing brands.

fields.name: address
fields.description: This user's address.

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.description: AccountUser's date of birth.

fields.name: label
fields.description: A free text field to help identifying the customer (e.g. for a job title).

fields.name: isActive
fields.description: Whether this user is active.

fields.name: details
fields.description: List of details linked to this user.

fields.name: permissions
fields.description: Holds information about the permissions of the current viewer.

fields.name: fullName
fields.description: The user's full name.

fields.name: preferredName
fields.description: The user's preferred name.

fields.name: portfolioIds
fields.description: List of portfolio ids that the user is linked to via their portfolio roles.

args.name: allowedBrandCodes
args.description: Optionally filter the user's portfolios to only return those linked to specified brands.

args.name: restrictToPublicFacingBrands
args.description: Optionally restrict the user portfolios to only return those linked to public facing brands.

fields.name: businesses
fields.description: List of businesses that the user has access to through their granted roles.

fields.name: alternativePhoneNumbers
fields.description: List of alternative phone numbers for the account user.

fields.name: hasFamilyIssues
fields.description: Whether there are family issues.

fields.name: isInHardship
fields.description: True if user is linked to an account with an active hardship agreement.

fields.name: accountUserRoles
fields.description: List of roles a user has for each account they're linked to.

args.name: accountNumber
args.description: Optionally filter the user's account roles to only return those linked to specific accounts.

fields.name: portfolioUserRoles
fields.description: List of roles a user has for each portfolio they're linked to.

args.name: portfolioNumber
args.description: Optionally filter the portfolio's user roles to only return those linked to a specific portfolio.

args.name: accountNumber
args.description: Return the user portfolio roles for this account's portfolio.

fields.name: consents
fields.description: Consents linked to this user.

fields.name: paymentMethods
fields.description: List payment instructions linked to this user.

args.name: validAt
args.description: Optionally return only payment instructions valid at the given datetime.

args.name: statuses
args.description: Optionally filter the payment instructions by status.

fields.name: accountUserMeta
fields.description: Additional user details, if they exist.

fields.name: externalIdAccountLinks
fields.description: List of currently active (LINKED) external vendor ID linkages for this user. Due to database constraints, each user can only have one active linkage per vendor.

Account.description: The account object can be one of several attached to a `Portfolio` (which usually has an `AccountUser` which is used to authenticate access to the `Portfolio`'s accounts). Typically a person has a single account attached to a portfolio and properties on the same account will appear on the same bill. Where separate bills are desired, multiple accounts  are created and added to the same portfolio of accounts.

fields.name: number
fields.description: A code that uniquely identifies the account.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of the account.

fields.name: paymentSchedules
fields.description: The schedules that describe how we would expect to take payments for an account on a given month.

args.name: includeDormant
args.description: If false, we remove the currently valid schedule if it has already fulfilled its purpose and will have no further impact on customer payments, along with any schedules which are not valid from today.

args.name: ledgerType
args.description: The ledger to return transactions from.

args.name: ledgerNumber
args.description: The ledger number to return the payment schedules from.

fields.name: complaints
fields.description: The complaints associated with an account.

fields.name: brand
fields.description: The brand of the account.

fields.name: balance
fields.description: The current account balance.

args.name: includeAllLedgers
args.description: If this variable is set to true, the balance will be calculated based on the sum of balances on all ledgers configured to contribute to this account's balance. Otherwise, only the main ledger's balance will be fetched (legacy behaviour). If you are unsure, set this value to true.

fields.name: overdueBalance
fields.description: The current account overdue balance.

fields.name: urn
fields.description: Unique reference number from a 3rd party enrolment.

fields.name: billingName
fields.description: The billing name of the account.

fields.name: billingSubName
fields.description: The billing sub name of the account.

fields.name: billingEmail
fields.description: The billing email of the account.

fields.name: billingAddress
fields.description: The billing address of the account.

fields.name: splitBillingAddress
fields.description: List of billing address lines.

fields.name: address
fields.description:
The billing address of this account, stored in the new
libaddressinput-based format.

Note that `name` and `organization` are very unlikely to be
supplied here; the `billing_name` field on the account
itself is generally used for that purpose instead.

fields.name: portfolio
fields.description: The portfolio this account is linked to.

fields.name: ledgers
fields.description: Ledgers provide the foundation of bookkeeping functionality. Similar to a bank account, they allow us to keep track of financial activity on a particular customer account.

args.name: ledgerNumber
args.description: The ledger to return by its number.

args.name: includeDebtLedgers
args.description: If this variable is set to false, debt ledgerswill be filtered out of the ledgers list.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Metadata associated with the account.

fields.name: canRequestRefund
fields.description: Whether the account can request a credit refund.

fields.name: requestRefundEligibility
fields.description: Details about the eligibility status for requesting a refund.

args.name: ledgerNumber
args.description: The ledger number to check refund eligibility.

fields.name: payments
fields.description: The payments made into an account from a payment instruction.

args.name: ledgerNumber
args.description:
**WARNING: Will be mandatory in future versions**

The number of the ledger to return payments from.

args.name: reason
args.description: Filter for payments that were taken for a particular reason.

args.name: includePromises
args.description: Whether to include payment promises (of any kind: made, broken or fulfilled) or not. The default is to include them.

fields.name: repayments
fields.description: The repayments that have been requested for this account.

args.name: statuses
args.description: Only return repayments whose status matches one of these statuses.

fields.name: paymentPlans
fields.description: The payment plans that have been created for this account.

fields.name: paginatedPaymentForecast
fields.description: Paginated payment forecasts for an account. Starts from today's date (inclusive). The interface supports `last` but does not guarantee 'lastness'.

args.name: dateTo
args.description: The end date to retrieve forecasts for. This end date is inclusive.

args.name: ledgerNumber
args.description: The number of the ledger to return payment forecasts from. Defaults to the main ledger when no number is passed.

fields.name: referrals
fields.description: The referrals created by this account.

args.name: status
args.description: Only return referrals with the specified status. Return all referrals by default.

fields.name: referralsCreated
fields.description: Number of referrals created by this account.

fields.name: rewards
fields.description: The rewards applied to this account.

fields.name: activeReferralSchemes
fields.description: The referral schemes currently active for this account.

fields.name: transactions
fields.description: Fetch transactions that have taken place on the account.

args.name: transactionTypes
args.description: Include only these specific transaction types in the result.

args.name: transactionTypesExcluded
args.description: Exclude these specific transaction types from the result.

args.name: ledgerNumber
args.description: The number of the ledger to return transactions from.

args.name: includeAllLedgers
args.description: In a multi-ledger scenario, include all ledgers' transactions if `true`. Analogously, if `false` and no `ledgerId` or `ledger_number` argument is present, the list of transactions defaults to the main ledger (legacy behaviour).

args.name: fromDate
args.description:
Optional date representing the beginning of the postedDate range filter.
This date value is inclusive.

args.name: toDate
args.description:
Optional date representing the end of the postedDate range filter.
This date value is exclusive.

args.name: orderBy
args.description: The order in which to return the transactions.

fields.name: annualStatements
fields.description: Fetch annual statements for the account.

fields.name: bills
fields.description: Fetch issued bills (invoices/statements) for the account.

args.name: includeBillsWithoutPDF
args.description: Include bills without PDFs.

args.name: includeOpenStatements
args.description:
Include open statements. This flag needs to be used along with
includeBillsWithoutPDF=false otherwise results will prove unexpected.

args.name: includeHeldStatements
args.description: Include held statements within the results.

args.name: includeHistoricStatements
args.description: Include pre-Kraken / historical statements within the results.

args.name: onlyCurrentEmail
args.description: Only include bills emailed to the current user's email.

args.name: ledgerNumber
args.description: The number of a ledger for the bill. When provided, only period- or item-based documents for this account that are linked to that ledger are returned; collective bills and legacy bills are excluded. Historic/pre-Kraken bills are unaffected by this filter and are included or excluded solely by includeHistoricStatements.

args.name: fromDate
args.description: Optional date representing the beginning of the search results. This date value is inclusive.

args.name: toDate
args.description: Optional date representing the end of the search results. This date value is exclusive.

args.name: issuedFromDate
args.description: Optional date representing the beginning of the search results based on issued date. This date value is inclusive.

args.name: issuedToDate
args.description: Optional date representing the end of the search results based on issued date. This date value is exclusive.

args.name: orderBy
args.description: The order in which to return the bills.

fields.name: billingOptions
fields.description: Information about the account's billing cycle.

fields.name: bill
fields.description: Fetch a specific issued bill (invoice/statement) for the account.

args.name: id
args.description: The unique identifier of the bill.

args.name: identifier
args.description: The unique identifier of the bill. A pending billing document will not have an identifier yet.                 And not all finalized billing documents will have an identifier. Prefer using the id field unless advised by Kraken.

args.name: ledgerNumber
args.description: The number of the ledger for the bill. When provided, only the period- or item-based documentfor this account that is linked to that ledger is returned; pre-kraken, collective bills, and legacy bills are excluded.

fields.name: directDebitInstructions
fields.description: The direct debit instructions of the account

args.name: statuses
args.description: Filter the direct debit instructions by status

fields.name: paymentMethods
fields.description: The payment instructions of the account.

args.name: statuses
args.description: Filter the payment instructions by status.

fields.name: events
fields.description: The account events that were recorded for the account.

args.name: eventTypes
args.description: Filter the account events by their type.

fields.name: applications
fields.description: Applications by this account to become our customer. More recent applications will be listed first.

args.name: status
args.description: The account application status.

fields.name: accountType
fields.description: The type of account.

fields.name: business
fields.description: Business info related to a business account.

fields.name: commsDeliveryPreference
fields.description: The method the account has specified they prefer we contact them.

fields.name: documentAccessibility
fields.description: The document accessibility preference of the account.

fields.name: references
fields.description: Account references linked to this account.

fields.name: fileAttachments
fields.description: Files attached to this account.

fields.name: paginatedFileAttachments
fields.description: Files attached to this account.

args.name: id
args.description: File attachment id.

args.name: category
args.description: File attachment category.

fields.name: maximumRefund
fields.description: The maximum amount a customer is allowed to request as a refund and the reason why that's the maximum amount.

args.name: ledgerNumber
args.description: The ledger number to calculate maximum refund for.

fields.name: campaigns
fields.description: The campaigns associated with an account.

fields.name: isInHardship
fields.description: True if there is an active Hardship Agreement for this account. False otherwise.

fields.name: activeHardshipAgreements
fields.description: List of active hardship agreements for the user when is_in_hardship is True.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Notes for the account.

fields.name: contributionAgreements
fields.description: Contribution agreements for account.

fields.name: assistanceAgreements
fields.description: Assistance agreements for account.

fields.name: debtCollectionProceedings
fields.description: Debt collection proceedings for account.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The datetime that the account was originally created.

fields.name: preferredLanguageForComms
fields.description: The language that the account preferred for communications.

fields.name: consents
fields.description: Consents linked to this account.

fields.name: reminders
fields.description: The reminders associated with an account.

fields.name: properties
fields.description: Properties linked to the account now and in the future.

args.name: activeFrom
args.description: Returns properties that have an effective period that ends at or after the given activeFrom datetime.

fields.name: marketSupplyAgreements
fields.description: Retrieve all market supply agreements under this account. Note that when `active` is set to false it actually means 'return both active and inactive agreements'.

args.name: id
args.description: Filter the list to the agreement with this id.

args.name: active
args.description: Whether to return active agreements only.

fields.name: canModifyPayments
fields.description: Data about whether or not an account can modify their payments.

fields.name: consentData
fields.description: A JSON object containing consent information.

fields.name: paymentSchedule
fields.description: Current active payment schedule detail of the account.

fields.name: paymentInstructions
fields.description: The payment instructions of the account

args.name: statuses
args.description: Filter the payment instructions by status

fields.name: paymentPreferences
fields.description: The customer's preferred payment methods.

fields.name: availableDemandResponseCampaignsForRegistration
fields.description: A list of available demand response campaigns in which this account can participate.

fields.name: demandResponseCampaignParticipants
fields.description: The demand response campaigns associated with an account.

fields.name: canBeWithdrawn
fields.description: Check whether the account can be withdrawn.

fields.name: hasActiveAgreement
fields.description: Check whether the account has an active energy agreement.

fields.name: hasFutureAgreement
fields.description: Check whether the account has a future energy agreement.

fields.name: lifelineAgencyApplication
fields.description: Lifeline Agency application.

fields.name: address
fields.description: The address of the property, formatted into a single string.

fields.name: richAddress
fields.description: Property rich address.

fields.name: splitAddress
fields.description: List of address lines.

fields.name: label
fields.description: An optional label for the property.

fields.name: occupancyPeriods
fields.description: Time periods during which the property is associated with an account. Useful to display information about house-moves, as performing a move out of a property will set the end date for the occupancy period.

fields.name: coordinates
fields.description: Coordinates for the property, useful for displaying the property on a map.

fields.name: embeddedNetwork
fields.description: The embedded network this property belongs to, if any.

fields.name: measurements
fields.description: Measurements at a property

args.name: startAt
args.description: Defaults to the far past which will return the oldest readings.

args.name: endAt
args.description: Defaults to the far future which will return the most recent readings.

args.name: startOn
args.description: Earliest date to return measurements from.

args.name: endOn
args.description: Latest date to return measurements for (inclusive).

args.name: timezone
args.description: Timezone to use for grouping. Defaults to Kraken localtime.

args.name: utilityFilters
args.description: Filters for each utility being measured.

fields.name: postcode
fields.description: The postcode of the property.

fields.name: electricitySupplyPoints
fields.description: All the electricity supply points at this property.

fields.name: electricityGenerationPoints
fields.description: All the electricity generation points at this property.

fields.name: gasSupplyPoints
fields.description: All the gas supply points at this property.

fields.name: spin
fields.description: The supply point identification number.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The meter point status.

fields.name: agreements
fields.description: The Agreements linked to this electricity supply point.

fields.name: supplyDetails
fields.description: Historical supply details list for this account, ordered by the valid_from date in descending order.

fields.name: latestCosLossProcess
fields.description: The latest switch-out process active on this account.

fields.name: latestMoveOutProcess
fields.description: The latest move-out process active on this account.

fields.name: activeAmperageChangeProcess
fields.description: A currently active amperage change process.

fields.name: canApplyAmperageChange
fields.description: An indication of whether a customer can apply for the change themselves.

fields.name: canUpdateMoveInDate
fields.description: An indication of whether the move-in date can be updated.

fields.name: canUpdateMoveOutDate
fields.description: An indication of whether the move-out date can be updated.

fields.name: newAmperageOptions
fields.description: A list of the possible amperage values to select.

fields.name: earliestAmperageChangeDate
fields.description: The earliest date in which an amperage change can be performed.

fields.name: intervalReadings
fields.description: Return the monthly confirmed readings associated with a given Supply Point. Restricted by the Agreement Periods.

args.name: productCode
args.description: Base cost estimations on this product.

args.name: sortOrder
args.description: Sort order of the readings.

args.name: endAt
args.description: Defaults to the far future which will return the most recent readings.

args.name: startAt
args.description: Defaults to the far future which will return the oldest readings.

fields.name: halfHourlyReadings
fields.description: Fetch Half Hourly Readings for this SPIN from and to a given datetime of any version. Restricted by the Agreement Periods.

args.name: fromDatetime
args.description: Search for Readings where the start of the period is greater than or equal to this datetime.

args.name: toDatetime
args.description: Search for Readings where the start of the period is less than this datetime.

args.name: productCode
args.description: Base cost estimations on this product.

fields.name: nextReadingDate
fields.description: Date of when the next reading will be collected by the grid operator.

fields.name: nextNextReadingDate
fields.description: Date of when the reading after the next reading will be collected by the grid operator.

fields.name: readingDateDayOfMonth
fields.description: The day of the month on which the reading is collected.

fields.name: supplyPeriods
fields.description: The supply periods for the supply point.

fields.name: references
fields.description: References for the electricity supply point.

fields.name: meters
fields.description: The electricity meters associated with this supply point.

fields.name: contractedCapacity
fields.description: The calculated capacity.

fields.name: contractedCapacityOld
fields.description: (Soon to be deprecated) The calculated capacity based on the agreement.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the supply point.

fields.name: marketName
fields.description: The market this supply point belongs to.

fields.name: externalIdentifier
fields.description: The external identifier of the supply point.

fields.name: readings
fields.description: Get readings from a readable device e.g., a supply point, device, or register.

args.name: startAt
args.description: The inclusive start of the time period as an isoformatted string.

args.name: endAt
args.description: The exclusive end of the time period as an isoformatted string.

args.name: readingType
args.description: Get readings of only this type.

args.name: timeGranularity
args.description: Time buckets to group readings into.

args.name: timezone
args.description: The timezone in which to return readings.

args.name: units
args.description: Optional filter to return readings only for specific list of units or unit (e.g., KILOWATT_HOURS, METERS_CUBED, LITER, etc.).

fields.name: property
fields.description: The supply point's property.

fields.name: devices
fields.description: Get list of devices under a supply point.

args.name: deviceIdentifiers
args.description: The ids of the devices to get. If not provided, all devices for the supply point will be returned.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The recorded value for this reading.

fields.name: units
fields.description: This reading's units.

fields.name: intervalStart
fields.description: The inclusive start of this reading's interval.

fields.name: intervalEnd
fields.description: The exclusive end of this reading's interval.

fields.name: source
fields.description: The source of this reading if applicable.

fields.name: quality
fields.description: The quality of this reading if applicable.

fields.name: startDate
fields.description: Start date of the consumption period.

fields.name: endDate
fields.description: End date of the consumption period.

fields.name: quantity
fields.description: Amount of energy or resource consumed.

fields.name: usageCost
fields.description: Cost for the consumption usage in minor currency units.

fields.name: supplyCharge
fields.description: Supply charge amount in minor currency units.

fields.name: gridOperatorCode
fields.description: Grid operator.

fields.name: regionOfOperation
fields.description: Region of operation.

fields.name: band
fields.description: The product rate band information.

fields.name: validFrom
fields.description: Rate validity start.

fields.name: validTo
fields.description: Rate validity end.

fields.name: unitType
fields.description: What are we charging for (kWhs consumed, Days on supply, etc).

fields.name: pricePerUnit
fields.description: Price per unit.

fields.name: durationMonths
fields.description: Duration in months for discount product rates.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the agreement.

fields.name: validFrom
fields.description: The start datetime of the agreement.

fields.name: validTo
fields.description: The end datetime of the agreement.

fields.name: agreedFrom
fields.description: The datetime the agreement was entered.

fields.name: agreedTo
fields.description: The datetime the agreement was terminated.

fields.name: isRevoked
fields.description: Whether the agreement is revoked.

fields.name: rescissionDeadlineAt
fields.description: The deadline datetime for rescinding the agreement.

fields.name: addonRates
fields.description: List of add-on rates on the agreement.

fields.name: params
fields.description: The agreement additional params.

fields.name: code
fields.description: The tariff's product code.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: The tariff's product's human-readable name.

fields.name: gridOperatorCode
fields.description: The grid operator code for this specific tariff.

fields.name: productParams
fields.description: A JSON object containing extra information.

fields.name: tiers
fields.description: A list of tiers available to the tariff.

fields.name: amperage
fields.description: Supply details capacity in Amperes.

fields.name: kva
fields.description: Supply details capacity in kVA.

fields.name: kw
fields.description: Supply details capacity in kW.

fields.name: validFrom
fields.description: Date when supply details came into effect. This can be before the account came on supply.

fields.name: limit
fields.description: The maximum number of points the viewer gets for requests per hour.

fields.name: remainingPoints
fields.description: The remaining points for the viewer in one hour time limit.

fields.name: usedPoints
fields.description: The points used so far in one hour time limit.

fields.name: ttl
fields.description: Time To Live: UNIX timestamp when the viewer will get a new allowance of points.

fields.name: isBlocked
fields.description: Whether the viewer has been blocked due to spending all its allowed points.

QueryComplexityInputType.description: Information about the complexity of the query.

fields.name: complexityValue
fields.description: The complexity of the query.
